skill.name: p-image
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Pruna P-Image models through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Run fast Pruna P-Image generation, LoRA styling, and image editing workflows from an AI assistant.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are confirmed as intentional CLI usage for the inference.sh service, not hidden malware. The network findings are expected for a cloud image-generation skill, while the weak cryptography findings appear to be false positives with no cryptographic operation shown.

medium_findings.title: External Cloud CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run inference.sh CLI commands for Pruna image models. This is the stated purpose of the skill, but it executes external commands and depends on a third-party cloud service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct evidence of external CLI execution. The intent appears legitimate because every command targets the documented image-generation service.

medium_findings.title: User Image Data Sent to External Service
medium_findings.description: The editing examples pass remote image URLs to the Pruna image-editing app. This may disclose user image locations and prompts to inference.sh or the underlying model provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples explicitly include image URLs as input to a cloud model command. This is expected functionality, but it creates a real privacy consideration.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, an example image, and placeholder user image URLs. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or suspicious destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links and sample assets for the named provider. I did not find evidence that secrets or local data are transmitted to those links.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations do not show hashing, encryption, or signature verification. They appear to be substring matches inside descriptive text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both cited lines contain product description or model preset text, not cryptographic code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or related weak algorithm use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell blocks that run `infsh app run` and related commands. These commands should remain limited to the documented inference.sh workflow and should not include unreviewed shell fragments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell blocks are clear command-execution patterns. They are bounded to `infsh` usage in context, which lowers maliciousness but not operational risk.

content.user_title: Generate Fast Images with Pruna P-Image
content.value_statement: Image creation often needs fast iteration across prompts, styles, and edits. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code run Pruna P-Image workflows through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Pruna P-Image text-to-image jobs through the inference.sh CLI.
Uses P-Image-LoRA presets for styled image generation.
Runs P-Image-Edit for prompt-based image editing.
Supports multi-image compositing with remote image URLs.
Shows aspect ratio options for square, landscape, portrait, and custom outputs.
Lists related Pruna image apps available through inference.sh.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends prompts and image URLs to an external cloud service.
Does not provide local image generation or offline model execution.
Model availability, pricing, and output quality depend on inference.sh and Pruna.

use_cases.title: Rapid Concept Art
use_cases.description: Create many visual directions for characters, scenes, or product concepts from short prompts.

use_cases.title: Marketing Asset Drafts
use_cases.description: Generate product shots, campaign visuals, and social image drafts with controlled aspect ratios.

use_cases.title: AI Workflow Prototyping
use_cases.description: Test Pruna model variants, LoRA styles, and editing flows from a repeatable CLI workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a modern desk setup in soft morning light using Pruna P-Image.

prompt_templates.title: Set Size and Style
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 16:9 image of a mountain cabin at sunset. Use a cinematic style and explain the selected P-Image settings.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a LoRA Preset
prompt_templates.prompt: Use P-Image-LoRA to generate a portrait with the anime-2-5d preset. Keep the prompt suitable for a public portfolio.

prompt_templates.title: Edit and Composite Images
prompt_templates.prompt: Use P-Image-Edit to combine these image URLs into a clean product collage with a neutral studio background.

output_examples.input: Generate a square product photo of white sneakers with studio lighting.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs a Pruna P-Image job and returns the generated image result with the selected square aspect ratio.

output_examples.input: Change this image background to a beach scene.
output_examples.output: The assistant submits the image URL and edit prompt to P-Image-Edit, then provides the edited image output.

output_examples.input: Create a stylized portrait using the neon-punk preset.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses P-Image-LoRA with the requested preset and returns a styled portrait result.

best_practices: Review prompts before sending private or sensitive information to the cloud service.
Use explicit aspect ratios when the final placement is known.
Start with short prompts, then refine lighting, subject, style, and composition.
anti_patterns: Do not submit confidential images unless the service terms allow that use.
Do not assume generated images are licensed for every commercial use.
Do not mix unrelated model apps in one request without checking the desired output.

faq.question: What does this skill require?
faq.answer: It requires the inference.sh CLI and access to the Pruna P-Image apps.

faq.question: Can it generate images from text?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for text-to-image generation with Pruna P-Image.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It can call P-Image-Edit with image URLs and an editing prompt.

faq.question: Does it support styles?
faq.answer: Yes. P-Image-LoRA examples show preset styles such as pixel art, neon punk, and cinematic looks.

faq.question: Does it run models locally?
faq.answer: No. It runs cloud jobs through inference.sh and returns the service output.

faq.question: Is it safe for private images?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow can send image URLs and prompts to an external service.
